problem text: Let $X$ and $Y$ be two random variables with variances equal to $9$ and $16$, respectively. Determine the standard deviation of $X+Y$ given that $\rho(X,Y) = -3/8$.
problem solution: We know that $ \text{Var}(X+Y) = \text{Var}(X) + \text{Var}(Y) + 2\text{Cov}(X,Y) = 9 + 16 + 2\left(-\frac{3}{8}\right)\sqrt{9}\sqrt{16} = 16 $, hence the standard deviation of $ X+Y $ is $ \sqrt{16} = 4 $.
